perturbation.original_text: If, for any Payment Date, the amounts deposited under Section 4.3(b) for the Collection Period exceed the sum of (i) Collections for the Collection Period, plus (ii) Acquisition Amounts for the Payment Date, the Indenture Trustee or the Note Paying Agent will pay to Cellco from Available Funds in the Collection Account an amount equal to the excess within two (2) Business Days after Cellco's direction, but no later than the Payment Date.
perturbation.changed_text: If, for any Payment Date, the amounts deposited under Section 4.3(b) for the Collection Period exceed the sum of (i) Collections for the Collection Period, plus (ii) Acquisition Amounts for the Payment Date, the Indenture Trustee or the Note Paying Agent will retain the excess in the Collection Account, and it will be used to offset future deposits by the servicer, and it will not be paid to Cellco from Available Funds in the Collection Account.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for Cellco to be paid back for excess deposits, whereas the new text does not, creating a contradiction. The contract mentions in other sections the servicer should be paid within two days which is being directly affected with this perturbation. This also creates an In-text contradiction in Section 4.3(c)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Reconciliation of Deposits. If Cellco is the Servicer and for any Payment Date, the sum of (i) Collections for the Collection Period, plus (ii) Acquisition Amounts for the Payment Date, exceeds the amounts deposited under Section 4.3(b) for the Collection Period, Cellco will deposit an amount equal to the excess into the Collection Account on the second Business Day before the Payment Date. If, for any Payment Date, the amounts deposited under Section 4.3(b) for the Collection Period exceed the sum of (i) Collections for the Collection Period, plus (ii) Acquisition Amounts for the Payment Date, the Indenture Trustee or the Note Paying Agent will retain the excess in the Collection Account, and it will be used to offset future deposits by the servicer, and it will not be paid to Cellco from Available Funds in the Collection Account. If requested by the Indenture Trustee, Cellco will provide reasonable supporting details for its calculation of the amounts to be deposited or paid under this Section 4.3(c).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states that Cellco would be paid back excess deposits. The changed text states the excess will be retained and used to offset future deposits, not paid to Cellco. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text in this case, affirms the retention and offsetting of future deposits by the servicer, which contradicts the original allowance for Cellco to be paid back. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, creating inconsistent obligations within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The Depositor will pay the expenses to perform its obligations under this Agreement and the Issuer's and the Indenture Trustee's reasonable expenses to perfect the Issuer's interest in the Depositor Transferred Property and to enforce the Depositor's obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Depositor is not responsible for any expenses to perform its obligations under this Agreement, that responsibility will be of the Servicer, including the Issuer's and the Indenture Trustee's reasonable expenses to perfect the Issuer's interest in the Depositor Transferred Property and to enforce the Depositor's obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Depositor will pay the expenses to perform its obligations whereas the changed text now states that the Depositor is not responsible and that the responsiblity now falls to the servicer. This introduces an in-text contradiction to Section 5.2(d), which refers to the Depositor paying all taxes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Payment of Taxes. The Depositor will pay all taxes levied or assessed on the Trust Property.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Depositor is *not* responsible for expenses to perform its obligations, shifting the responsibility to the Servicer. However, the contradicted text (d) explicitly states, 'The Depositor will pay all taxes levied or assessed on the Trust Property.' This creates a direct conflict because the changed text denies the Depositor's responsibility for expenses, while (d) specifically requires the Depositor to pay taxes, which are a type of expense. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: The Issuer and the Indenture Trustee are authorized to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Servicer, as attorney-in-fact or otherwise, all documents, and to do all other acts or things necessary or advisable to effect the termination and replacement of the Servicer.
perturbation.changed_text: The Issuer and the Indenture Trustee do not have the right or authorization to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Servicer, as attorney-in-fact or otherwise, all documents, and to do all other acts or things necessary or advisable to effect the termination and replacement of the Servicer; such authorization remains solely with Cellco as the current servicer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text authorizes the Issuer and the Indenture Trustee to act on behalf of the Servicer, whereas the new text explicitly denies them this authority. This creates a contradiction with Section 3.2(f), which initially grants the Issuer power of attorney to the Servicer. The text refers to Section 3.13, which discusses when to send notices, implying such notices need to be sent. This is now contradictive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.13 Notices to Obligors. Within ten (10) days following the earlier to occur of (i) a ratings downgrade by each of the Rating Agencies of Verizon to below investment grade, or (ii) a Servicer Termination Event, the Servicer will send a notice to all Obligors indicating (a) that their Receivables have been assigned to the Issuer, and (b)(x) if Cellco has not been removed as Servicer, that the Obligors shall continue to make their payments as they had previously, or (y) if Cellco has been removed as Servicer, the name of the Successor Servicer and any new instructions with respect to their payments. In addition, if the Servicer Termination Event was as a result of the failure of the Marketing Agent to satisfy its obligation to make, or to cause the related Originators to make, required Upgrade Payments pursuant to Section 7.2(a)(i)(y), then Cellco shall also send a notice to (i) all Obligors who have a continuing right to an upgrade, indicating that Cellco has recently failed to make the necessary prepayments with respect to one or more of its customers in connection with an Upgrade Offer, and that if any Obligor chooses to upgrade and Cellco fails to make the related Upgrade Payment with respect to them, such Obligor will still be required to make payments on his or her original device payment plan agreement, but that such Obligor will have a corresponding recoupment right against his or her new device payment plan agreement with Verizon Wireless, and (ii) all Obligors who had initiated upgrades under an Upgrade Offer, indicating that Cellco had failed to make the relevant Upgrade Payment, and stating that such Obligors will continue to have an obligation to make payments on their original device payment plan agreements, but will have a corresponding right of recoupment against their new device payment plan agreements with Verizon Wireless.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly denies the Issuer and Indenture Trustee the authority to act on behalf of the Servicer, which directly contradicts the requirement in Section 3.13 for the Servicer to send notices. If the Issuer and Indenture Trustee cannot act on behalf of the Servicer, there is a clear contradiction regarding the Servicer's responsibility to send the notices, and if they fail to do so or can't, the obligation is now unclear, creating an in-text contradiction.
